description:
You are a cloud-architect who has just finished migrating the last micro-service of the “cloud-migrate” project into its new VPC.  
 The project lives in  

     /home/user/cloud-migrate  

 Inside that directory two text files already exist:

     1. /home/user/cloud-migrate/VERSION
        • Contains exactly one line – the current semantic version string:  
          1.2.3

     2. /home/user/cloud-migrate/CHANGELOG.md  
        • Holds the project’s past change-history and already starts with:  

          ## [1.2.3] - 2023-09-30  
          ### Added  
          - Initial deployment pipeline for staging.

 Task
 ----
 1. Perform a PATCH version bump (from 1.2.3 ➜ 1.2.4).  
 2. Record that bump in **both** files by:

    a. Replacing the single line in /home/user/cloud-migrate/VERSION so that it now reads exactly:  
       1.2.4

    b. Prepending the following block at the very top of /home/user/cloud-migrate/CHANGELOG.md (i.e. the new block must appear **before** the existing “## [1.2.3] …” section):

       ## [1.2.4] - 2023-10-18
       ### Changed
       - Migrated 'user-auth' microservice to new VPC architecture.

       Important strict formatting rules the automated tests will validate:  
       • The block must start with the literal characters “## [1.2.4] - 2023-10-18” (including the two leading hash symbols and a single space after them).  
       • Line-breaks must be Unix style (LF).  
       • There must be a blank line between the header line (“## [1.2.4] …”) and the “### Changed” line, exactly as shown above.  
       • There must NOT be any trailing spaces on any of the three lines in the new block.  
       • After the “- Migrated …” line a single newline is required so that the next line in the file begins the previous 1.2.3 section.

 3. For traceability, create a one-line log file called  

        /home/user/cloud-migrate/version_bump.log  

    that contains exactly the new version string followed by a newline:

        1.2.4

 Once finished, the directory should look like:

     cloud-migrate/
     ├── CHANGELOG.md   ← now begins with the 1.2.4 entry
     ├── VERSION        ← now says 1.2.4
     └── version_bump.log  ← newly created, single line “1.2.4”

 You may accomplish everything with a single terminal command if you like, but how you do it is up to you.  The automated tests will verify that:

 • /home/user/cloud-migrate/VERSION contains only “1.2.4”.
 • /home/user/cloud-migrate/version_bump.log exists and also contains only “1.2.4”.
 • /home/user/cloud-migrate/CHANGELOG.md begins with the exact three-line block shown above and the previous 1.2.3 section is still present below it, unchanged.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent:

 Directory: /home/user/cloud-migrate

 File: /home/user/cloud-migrate/VERSION  
 Contents (single line, LF newline):  
 1.2.3\n

 File: /home/user/cloud-migrate/CHANGELOG.md  
 Contents (exact, LF newlines):  
 ## [1.2.3] - 2023-09-30\n
 ### Added\n
 - Initial deployment pipeline for staging.\n

 Desired final state for grading:

 1. /home/user/cloud-migrate/VERSION  
    Contents:  
    1.2.4\n

 2. /home/user/cloud-migrate/CHANGELOG.md  
    Contents should start with:  
    ## [1.2.4] - 2023-10-18\n
    \n
    ### Changed\n
    - Migrated 'user-auth' microservice to new VPC architecture.\n
    \n
    ## [1.2.3] - 2023-09-30\n
    ### Added\n
    - Initial deployment pipeline for staging.\n

    (Everything after the 1.2.3 block, if any, must remain identical to its initial state.)

 3. /home/user/cloud-migrate/version_bump.log  
    Contents:  
    1.2.4\n

 The grader will check:
 • Exact file paths and names.
 • Exact byte-level match of the three files’ contents shown above.
 • Existence of only the files listed (no extra files introduced).
